Thanks [~giovanni.fumarola] for the patch.
A few comments:
* Can you fix the checstyle.
* Can we use Time.now instead of currentTimeMillis?
* Given that we access the response, I don't think we need to check if it's not null.
* No need to specify the type in the new of executor completion service in line 1071.
* Remove empty line 1094 in the interceptor.
* Do you need to close the completion service?
* For the exception management, should we actually throw the exceptions instead of logging and returning empty?
*

Thanks [~elgoiri] . 
 * Do you need to close the completion service?
No, there is no need to close them.
 * For the exception management, should we actually throw the exceptions instead of logging and returning empty?
No, the second part of the Future execution will catch the exception.

Thank you [~giovanni.fumarola] for patch.

Few comments

# Can we have a generic methods similar to FederationClientInterceptor#invokeConcurrent ? getClusterMetricsInfo and getContainers and others can reuse.
# IMHO better to send exception, than empty response when clusters are not available. Applications zero containers and  zero subcluster currently return empty.

Thanks [~bibinchundatt] for the comment.
I replied to 2) in the [comment|https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-8897?focusedCommentId=16655733&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-16655733 ]
I will continue to follow up on this pr.

Thanks [~giovanni.fumarola] for the original patch and [~slfan1989] for the final PR.

[~elgoiri] Thank you very much! 

Thanks everyone!

Thanks [~giovanni.fumarola] for the patch.
A few comments:
* Can you fix the checstyle.
* Can we use Time.now instead of currentTimeMillis?
* Given that we access the response, I don't think we need to check if it's not null.
* No need to specify the type in the new of executor completion service in line 1071.
* Remove empty line 1094 in the interceptor.
* Do you need to close the completion service?
* For the exception management, should we actually throw the exceptions instead of logging and returning empty?
*

Thanks [~elgoiri] . 
 * Do you need to close the completion service?
No, there is no need to close them.
 * For the exception management, should we actually throw the exceptions instead of logging and returning empty?
No, the second part of the Future execution will catch the exception.
